rationale.R1.2: Checking evidence-to-claim support requires tracing inferential steps: e.g., whether KL=0.92 vs. KL=0.47 logically entails 'better calibration,' and whether a single-family gain (code) logically establishes 'uniform improvement across all task families.' These are load-bearing logical moves embedded in the evidence narrative.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation is a direct, explicit audit of whether reported experimental results (Table 1 accuracy and KL values; Table 2 code-generation numbers) actually substantiate the paper's stated claims (calibration superiority, universal dominance on the cost-quality frontier). This is the singular scope of the task; failure here fully invalidates the delegation.
